title: Police and Forensics Teams Swarm Planes at Glasgow Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Police and forensic teams investigate two aircraft at Glasgow Airport on 2 February 2026, with no disruption to flights or passenger safety.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Police and forensic teams arrived at Glasgow Airport early Monday during probes linked to two aircraft. However, investigation continues without delay or disruption to flights. The presence of specialists followed standard procedure after initial reports reached authorities. The activity centred around the tarmac area where both aircraft remained grounded. sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: A welcome sign located inside Glasgow Airport ©Project Scotland Police and forensic teams arrived at Glasgow Airport early Monday during probes linked to two aircraft. However, investigation continues without delay or disruption to flights. The presence of specialists followed standard procedure after initial reports reached authorities. The activity centred around the tarmac area where both aircraft remained grounded. Officials maintained routine coordination throughout the morning hours. No statements were issued regarding suspects or causes. Procedures unfolded under structured protocols typical for such cases. Review of evidence began immediately upon the arrival of personnel. Measures taken aligned with established guidelines for aviation incidents. Examination focused on physical traces and recorded data from the units involved.
